description: Claim-level typed correspondence layered on the existing Principia Atlas. Lean is the validity boundary; this registry records correspondence status without collapsing literary expression into theorem text.

status_vocabulary.interpretive: philosophical or synthetic reading grounded in but not identical to formal results
status_vocabulary.poetic: intentionally non-propositional material outside theorem certification

entries.claim: The source-to-readout half constructs a Hermitian rank-one density from a normalized pure-state vector and lowers it through a fixed observer response into a Born/Hermitian-compatible resolution certificate.
entries.atlas_note: First of two complementary Gleason-facing half-bridges. The construction is forward and preserves upstream Hermiticity without strengthening the observer certificate.

entries.claim: Global-phase-related vectors have the same pure-state density and the same fixed-kernel observer certificate; distinct normalized vectors collide under lowering.
entries.atlas_note: This is the proved non-injectivity of observer lowering.

entries.claim: An arbitrary complete-frame readout system reconstructs a Hermitian readout certificate.
entries.atlas_note: The implication is formally false, not awaiting proof.

entries.claim: An arbitrary normalized observer-resolution certificate forces Hermiticity or reconstructs the full upstream state.
entries.atlas_note: The failure is information loss across the observer boundary, not a missing certificate field.

entries.claim: The readout-to-representation half constructs a Hermitian sesquilinear representation when explicit cross laws are supplied, or from a resolution certificate whose retained matrix is explicitly Hermitian.
premises: Explicit conjugate-linear/linear Hermitian cross laws, or a retained reduced-state matrix certified Hermitian.
entries.atlas_note: Second complementary Gleason-facing half-bridge. It represents the certified observer-level values and is not an inverse recovering the originating source.

entries.claim: Complex operator expectations scale by conjugate(a)*a and are invariant under certified global phase; real a^2 scaling is not the complex law.
entries.atlas_note: The exact kernel supports the phase-faithful correction without certifying the PS axiom as derived.

entries.claim: The appendix gives a human mathematical derivation under PS-C1--PS-C6; the Lean companion supplies the two finite half-bridges and their proved observer boundary without certifying the appendix theorem itself.
entries.atlas_note: The broader appendix argument remains human mathematics; neither formal half-bridge is mislabeled as the classical theorem.

entries.claim: Zero-defect convergence identifies the Hilbert cross-section; Born identification additionally consumes a separate uniqueness certificate.
premises: Defect continuity and convergence to zero.
entries.atlas_note: Hilbert collapse alone does not select a probability functional.

entries.claim: Reading non-contextuality as the fixed-point condition of reflective collapse is an interpretive synthesis of the conditional theorem.
entries.atlas_note: The prose reach is preserved but not presented as a further kernel identity.

entries.claim: Born as the p=2 slice is interpretive; the underlying temporal becoming is separately kernel-certified by Cacophony-facing cost, staged transport, and conditional convergence results. Physical decoherence/noise is the synthetic specialization.
entries.atlas_note: The scholium distinguishes the exact general temporal arrow, exact observer lowering, and the interpretive physical bridge between them.

entries.claim: Operatio is intentional operator-poetry and a semantic carrier, not a proposition awaiting theorem certification.
entries.atlas_note: Formal silence does not reject or flatten the operator-poetic register.

entries.claim: Finite environmental partial trace preserves trace and every represented local-observer expectation while lowering a joint operator to a reduced observer operator.
entries.atlas_note: This is an exact reduction/regrouping theorem, not a certified temporal channel or distinguishability monotonicity law.

entries.claim: Decoherence and noise continue the observer arrow through successive loss, quotient, or stabilization.
entries.atlas_note: The physical quantum specialization is functionally interpretive: an operational, testable map rather than a kernel identity. The general temporal cost-and-transport arrow, partial-trace reduction, and phase-collision boundary are exact or explicitly conditional.

entries.claim: Simultaneous finite-support compression obeys the certified Lp norm-fracture bound, and the positive diagonal witness has an exact strictly positive symbolic/geometric path-cost gap.
entries.atlas_note: This is the Cost of Cacophony-facing geometric obstruction: compression regime and support geometry determine a certified cost boundary.

entries.claim: A directed staged path has a telescoped per-step displacement bound, and each certified JKO step pays its scaled transport cost from free-energy decrease.
entries.atlas_note: Time supplies an ordered transport coordinate with explicit accumulated cost; this is not merely literary temporal language.

entries.claim: The staged trajectory converges when its per-step bounds are summable in a complete observer metric, or when nonnegative potential pays every increment at a positive linear descent rate.
premises: Summable per-step displacement bounds and completeness, or a nonnegative potential with positive linear descent control.
entries.atlas_note: Convergence is certified under explicit preservation/descent contracts, not inferred from staging alone.
